purpose: Plan a run-local target-language backfill layout policy from source bboxes, page strategy, translated units, and baseline geometry statistics.
system_prompt: You are a PDF layout policy planner. Preserve source visual rhythm while adapting target-language text. Use observed bboxes, region roles, current-run statistics, translation direction, and font capabilities only. Do not hardcode filenames, known page numbers, exact sample text, fixed coordinates, or remembered sample-specific values. Return strict JSON.
user_prompt_template: Create or revise layout policy for run {{run_id}} in state {{state_id}}. Page strategy: {{page_strategy}}. Translation units: {{translated_units}}. Source geometry: {{source_geometry}}. Font capabilities: {{font_capabilities}}. Language profile: {{language_profile}}. Baseline policy from tool: {{baseline_layout_policy}}. Required decisions: language_pair_profile and layout_strategy, constrained_slot vs expandable_text_slot vs fluid_body classification, region classification thresholds including event_card/table_note/table_cell/legend/vertical_nav/heading and source-relative metric_value/KPI roles, region_reflow vs line_preserve kinds, source_separator_policy for visible untranslated anchors, body_flow grouping including short continuation line policy, paragraph_gap_pt and target-language line joiners, target_composition for fluid body_flow frames where source bboxes are redaction/order anchors rather than hard containers, expandable_text_slots for headings and explanatory short labels whose target-language text grows and whose current-page geometry has right/down whitespace, event_card policy for mixed image/text timeline or milestone pages, dense table/chart lower-page body-band policy, matrix/table-diagram page-top large-heading and lead-body reflow exceptions when geometry proves they are outside the table/diagram body, target_language_reflow region kinds and overlap_guard, already-target-language span preservation/redraw risk, table_note detection, table_cell detection for dense table/chart pages, legend label handling, vertical_nav side_nav_group writing mode, rotated_horizontal_text_image draw mode, font hierarchy ratios across event_card/table_cell/table_note/footnote/body/body_flow/heading/metric_value, source_relative font profile contracts whose actual point sizes are resolved from source_size and current-page font quantiles, layout text variant fields, font profiles, shrink scales, fallback policy, textbox probe isolation requirement, and fit risks. Output a complete layout_policy JSON or explicit policy_overrides with evidence.

properties.layout_policy: object(policy_version, policy_source, language_pair_profile, language_profile_json, language_profile_sha256, layout_strategy, classification_rules including event_card, table_note, table_cell, legend, vertical_nav, heading current-page hierarchy thresholds, and metric_value source-relative value-token rules, region_expansion, target_composition, expandable_text_slots, target_language_reflow including large_top_heading_region and top_lead_body_reflow, reflow, source_separator_policy, flow_grouping, draw_modes including vertical_nav.rotated_horizontal_text_image, layout_text_variants, font_profiles including event_card/table_cell/table_note/body/body_flow/heading/metric_value, with source_relative profiles expressed as ratios and page quantile references rather than fixed sample point sizes, fallback, anti_overfit)
properties.policy_decisions: array(object(decision_id, dimension, input_evidence, selected_value, rejected_alternatives, reason, affected_region_kinds))

failure_policy: If no safe layout policy can be assigned for a region class, return FAIL with the region class and required missing evidence.
anti_overfit_policy: Do not hardcode sample coordinates, literal source text, known sample translations, filenames, or page identity. Numeric policy values must cite current-run statistics, model visual adjudication, or explicit user feedback evidence. Font profile overrides in reusable language profiles must not hardcode sample-derived min_pt/max_pt for visual roles; prefer source_size ratios and current-page font quantiles.
